prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Look at my company's website. I'll give you a URL or paste a key page when yo

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-step imperative sequence (Look / Extract / Write / append / Show me) invites Claude to narrate the plan before executing any step.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body callout: prime the student that Claude may open with a plan narration — skim past it and look for the URL question.

risks_fired.evidence: Show me both files before saving — two files, one of which (CLAUDE.md) is a global config; body asks student to edit style.md where Claude missed something, confirming non-trivial review.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Scope the verification callout: 'Check one thing — did style.md name specific hex codes or vague adjectives? Generic = push back.' Reduces the cognitive load of reviewing two files.

risks_fired.evidence: append one line to my root CLAUDE.md — append verb is present but if CLAUDE.md has no existing Styling heading Claude may rewrite surrounding content when creating it.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low confidence; no action required unless a TODO is desired.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm setting up a daily agent that reads my challenge memory every morning and r

risks_fired.evidence: Ask me, one at a time: 1. … 2. … 3. … — numbered list is visible in the fence; catalog rule requires hiding the list for the structural fix to hold; body has no question-dump callout.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'DO NOT show me the question list. Ask question 1 only, wait for my answer, then proceed.' inside the fence — OR add a body callout: 'If Claude posts all three at once, answer in order or push back and ask it to go one at a time. Your call.'

risks_fired.evidence: I'm setting up a daily agent… — scene-setting opening sentence may prompt Claude to acknowledge the setup before asking question 1.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low confidence; body says 'Claude asks, you answer' which primes expected behavior implicitly.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read module-2/morning-agent/morning.md and run the job. Read the current state

risks_fired.evidence: Write the output to module-2/morning-agent/latest.html — no explicit overwrite instruction in the scheduled-task fenced block; on runs 2–7 Claude may pause and ask before overwriting the existing file.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Overwrite module-2/morning-agent/latest.html if it exists.' to the fenced block, or verify that morning.md generated in step 3 reliably contains this instruction and Claude reads it before deciding.

risks_fired.evidence: Daily-overwrite intent is delegated to morning.md; whether Claude reads that delegation faithfully across cold scheduled sessions varies.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low confidence given the delegation chain; the overwrite-anxiety fix above covers this simultaneously.
